reviewer: hand-reviewed (Opus) — headless first-visit drive with real streaming generation, top-k 'see inside', mobile+desktop overflow matrix, modern-web-guidance consulted

rubric.evidence: Headless first-visit drive (fresh profile, WASM, no GPU): clicked Download (~450 MB) → state 'ready' in 144s → sent a prompt → streamed a REAL coherent answer about primary/fundamental colours (15 tokens, 1st token 894 ms, 4.67 s total, 3.2 tok/s, WASM). Independent CDN verification of the same model (transformers.js 4.2.0, q8) produced coherent multi-prompt answers (colours, photosynthesis) — non-degenerate, no repeated-token garbage. Real onnx-community/ERNIE-4.5-0.3B-ONNX text-generation pipeline runs in models/ernie-4-5-0-3b/worker.js.
rubric.notes: ernie4_5 architecture; the class exists only in transformers.js >= 4.2.0, so worker.js pins 4.2.0 LOCALLY (isolated escape hatch; shared 3.7.5 unchanged; lib/model-cache.js is version-agnostic so auto-init still works).

rubric.evidence: Output is streamed assistant text with a readout (tokens, 1st-token latency, tok/s, backend). Content is on-topic and grammatical. One observed greedy run leaked a leading 'assistant' role marker before the answer (a small-model / chat-template artefact, not fabricated output).
rubric.notes: Honest small-model (0.36B) behaviour: fluent and on-topic but less reliable than a multi-billion-parameter model. Disclosed on the page. Follow-up: trim a stray leading role token in the streamer.

rubric.evidence: At-a-glance links the exact source model id (onnx-community/ERNIE-4.5-0.3B-ONNX) + Baidu base repo, names task text-generation, architecture ernie4_5, dtype q8 (WASM) / q4f16 (WebGPU), the pinned Transformers.js 4.2.0, download size, license Apache-2.0. Readout shows the real backend used.

Uses the shared createModelLoader (lib/model-loader.js). Fresh profile showed 'Download model (~450 MB)' (absent, no auto-download); a validated cached copy auto-inits on return. Clear-cache + storage via the shared loader.
rubric.notes: The 4.2.0 version pin is scoped to the worker; the version-agnostic model-cache still detects the cached model by modelId.

rubric.evidence: checking → downloading (% progress) → ready via role=status aria-live; generation shows a live 'Generating on the WASM…' status, a streaming caret, and a Stop button; failures surface as .status.err. Retry via the shared loader.

Driven headless: Send (streamed answer), Show next-token distribution (12 real top-k rows), system prompt, sampling controls (temperature/top_p/max tokens/greedy), example chips (EN + Chinese), Clear conversation, Stop. Labels + aria-live present; primary buttons >=44px.

Tokens stream from a real forward pass; the top-k panel runs one real raw forward pass over the current context and softmaxes the last-position logits. Absent model shows Download, never a canned reply.

role=status aria-live for load + generation; transcript aria-live=polite; labelled system/input textareas and sliders; details/summary for sampling; primary buttons >=44px. WCAG-AA design system (light+dark). Consulted the accessibility guide.
rubric.notes: Streaming updates use innerHTML on an aria-live region; content is escaped. Reduced-motion disables the caret animation.

rubric.evidence: Mobile 360x740 DPR3 + desktop 1280x800: 0px horizontal overflow on the index and all 4 use-case pages (measured after fixing long-token wrapping: .glance table-layout:fixed + overflow-wrap:anywhere, and p/li code wrapping). The API code block scrolls inside its own overflow-x:auto container.

Generation + tokenization + the top-k forward pass all run in models/ernie-4-5-0-3b/worker.js (off the main thread); tokens stream incrementally to the UI so the main thread stays responsive. Consulted break-up-long-tasks / identify-inp-causes. 0 console errors during the streaming drive.

guidanceConsulted.query: stream LLM tokens from a web worker without blocking the main thread / INP
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Run heavy work (model inference, long loops) off the main thread and yield / stream results incrementally so interactions stay responsive.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — all generation runs in a dedicated module worker and streams tokens via postMessage; the UI only appends escaped text per token. No synchronous inference on the main thread.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: Headless streaming drive produced 15 tokens with 894 ms first-token latency and 0 console errors while the page stayed interactive.

guidanceConsulted.query: avoid INP regressions when updating the DOM on every streamed token
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Keep per-interaction work small; avoid large synchronous DOM/layout thrash on the input-handling path.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — per-token updates set a single escaped text node + caret and scroll the transcript; the expensive work is in the worker, not the event handler.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: Token cadence ~3.2 tok/s on WASM with smooth streaming; no long-task console warnings observed in the drive.

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive chat + glance table without horizontal overflow on mobile
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use flex-wrap/grid intrinsic sizing and contain wide content; stop unbreakable tokens (model ids, code) from forcing page overflow.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — composer + controls flex-wrap; glance table uses table-layout:fixed with overflow-wrap:anywhere; inline code in prose wraps; the API code block scrolls inside overflow-x:auto.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: Mobile document overflow went from 23px (an unbreakable inline code token) to 0px after adding p/li code wrapping; verified across index + 4 use-case pages.

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible streaming output and labelled sampling controls
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce load + generation via role=status aria-live; label controls; respect reduced motion.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — role=status aria-live for load + generation, aria-live transcript, labelled textareas/sliders, reduced-motion caret.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: Drive reached ready and streamed with 0 console errors; contrast from the shared WCAG-AA design system.

openQuestions: Should the streamer strip a stray leading 'assistant' role token that ERNIE occasionally emits under greedy decoding?
Is a q4f16 WASM path worth offering as a smaller download, given fp16 compute is a WebGPU-only kernel on the WASM EP?

followUpGoals.goal: Trim a stray leading role marker ('assistant') from the streamed output when it appears, without altering the real token stream underneath.
Add a bilingual code-switching wild demo: mid-conversation, ask ERNIE to continue in the other language and visualise the switch.

summary: Genuinely new, distinct LLM family (Baidu ERNIE 4.5, ernie4_5 class) verified streaming real, coherent, non-degenerate text on-device (WASM q8, ~450 MB) via an isolated transformers.js 4.2.0 worker pin. Bilingual EN/ZH framing throughout; real top-k 'see inside'; honest Download state; mobile+desktop 0px overflow. Minor: a stray leading role token under greedy decoding (disclosed, follow-up filed). Not a relabel of any built LLM.
